pipeline: Semantic Analysis (name resolution, type checking, PIC validation, data hierarchy)
IR Lowering (Binder: bound nodes → IR instructions)

god_classes.concern: Lowering orchestration + 58 statement lowerers + expression evaluation + control flow
Type mapping + 100+ emission methods + expression evaluation + runtime method resolution
40+ statement binders + condition binding + validation + procedure resolution
Symbol collection + REDEFINES/RENAMES resolution + sign propagation + data hierarchy

ledger.title: Eliminate BoundExpression leakage from IR layer
ledger.description: 44 occurrences of Semantics.Bound.BoundExpression embedded in IR instructions (IrPerformTimes, IrFunctionCall, IrElementRef, IrRefModLocation, etc.). IR should be independent of bound tree. Expression evaluation deferred to CIL emitter instead of being lowered during Bind().
ledger.fix: Create IrExpression hierarchy. Lower all BoundExpression → IrExpression during Bind(). Eliminate ResolvedLocations parameter bundles. CilEmitter becomes pure IR→CIL translator.
ledger.resolution: Implemented in 4 stages. Stage 1: IrExpression types + Binder.LowerExpression + 19 unit tests. Stage 2: migrated all 8 IR instructions + added EmitIrExpression to CilEmitter. Stage 3: deleted EmitExpression/EmitIntrinsicCall/PreResolveExpressionLocations/ResolvedLocations, moved 4 enums to IR namespace. Stage 4: 13 reflection contract tests + 13 integration pipeline tests. Design doc: docs/ir/IR-Expression-Contract.md. Zero BoundExpression references in IR or CilEmitter.

ledger.title: Break Binder.cs god class into focused lowerers
ledger.description: 4,266-line class does everything: record layout, paragraph methods, ALTER slots, 58-case LowerStatement switch, expression evaluation. Single-responsibility violated.
ledger.fix: Split into: Binder (orchestrator) + LoweringContext + 8 focused lowerers.
ledger.resolution: Implemented in 5 stages. Binder reduced from 4,266 to 579 lines (-86%). 8 lowerers created: LocationResolver (169), ExpressionLowerer (181), ConditionLowerer (615), ControlFlowLowerer (1154), ArithmeticLowerer (292), DataMovementLowerer (335), FileIoLowerer (688), StringLowerer (240). LoweringContext (82) owns all shared state. No forwarding wrappers remain. Design doc: docs/binder/Binder-Decomposition.md. 596 unit + 287 integration + 95 NIST guard all pass.

ledger.title: Break CilEmitter.cs god class into focused emitters
ledger.description: 4,083-line class with 100+ private methods handling type/field mapping, instruction emission, expression evaluation, location handling, and runtime method resolution.
ledger.fix: Split into: CilEmitter (orchestrator) + EmissionContext + 10 focused emitters.
ledger.resolution: Implemented in 5 stages. CilEmitter reduced from 4,083 to 1,299 lines (-68%). 10 emitters created: CilLocationEmitter (290), CilExpressionEmitter (280), CilComparisonEmitter (310), CilArithmeticEmitter (340), CilDataEmitter (420), CilControlFlowEmitter (375), CilStringEmitter (465), CilFileIoEmitter (270), CilModuleSetup (skeleton), CilProgramStateEmitter (skeleton). EmissionContext (95) owns all shared state. No forwarding wrappers remain. Design doc: docs/cilemitter/CilEmitter-Decomposition.md. 765 unit + 287 integration + 95 NIST guard all pass.

ledger.title: Break BoundTreeBuilder.cs into focused binders
ledger.description: 4,428-line visitor with 40+ if-pattern-matched statements for dispatch. Mixes statement binding, condition binding, validation, and procedure resolution in one class.
ledger.fix: Split into: BoundTreeBuilder (orchestrator) + BindingContext + 9 focused binders.
ledger.resolution: Implemented in 5 stages. BoundTreeBuilder reduced from 4,428 to 234 lines (-95%). 9 binders created: ExpressionBinder (780), ConditionBinder (510), ArithmeticStatementBinder (475), DataStatementBinder (420), ControlFlowBinder (740), FileIoBinder (630), CallBinder (120), StringStatementBinder (480), ProcedureNameResolver (120). BindingContext (65) owns all shared state. No forwarding wrappers remain. Design doc: docs/boundtree/BoundTreeBuilder-Decomposition.md. 922 unit + 287 integration + 95 NIST guard all pass.

ledger.title: Convert BoundNode classes to sealed records
ledger.description: 81 sealed bound node classes use traditional constructor+property pattern instead of C# 13 records. BoundExpression.ResultType is mutable on what should be immutable nodes.
ledger.fix: Convert all BoundNode subclasses to sealed records with init-only properties. Compute ResultType during binding, not post-binding. Estimated ~300 lines of boilerplate eliminated.

ledger.title: Convert IR instructions to sealed records
ledger.description: 40+ IrInstruction subclasses are immutable classes with only properties. Should be sealed records for structural equality, pattern matching, reduced boilerplate.
ledger.fix: Convert all IrInstruction and IrLocation subclasses to sealed records. Already partially done (IrTemp, IrParameter use records).

ledger.title: Replace IrRuntimeCall stringly-typed dispatch
ledger.description: IrRuntimeCall uses string method names ("FileRuntime.Init", "Self.Entry") for dispatch. No compile-time verification, no type checking of arguments.
ledger.fix: Create typed IR instructions for each runtime call pattern (IrInitFileManager, IrCallEntryPoint, IrRegisterFileHandler). CilEmitter maps these to actual method calls.

ledger.title: Extract shared IStatementVisitor interface
ledger.description: FileStateValidator, BoundTreeValidator, ProcedureGraph.CollectTransferEdges, Binder.LowerStatement all pattern-match statement types with 15-58 case arms each. Duplicated dispatch logic.
ledger.fix: Create IBoundStatementVisitor<T> interface with Visit(BoundXxxStatement) for each type. Implement double-dispatch on BoundStatement.Accept(). Eliminates 4+ switch-on-type cascades.

ledger.title: Symbol hierarchy immutability (records + init-only)
ledger.description: DataSymbol has 23 mutable property setters. Symbol base class has mutable DeclaringScope and IsValid. Enables state inconsistency during multi-phase analysis.
ledger.fix: Convert to records with init-only properties. Use builder pattern for construction: new DataSymbol { ... } with required members.

ledger.title: Split SemanticBuilder into focused visitors
ledger.description: 1,477-line class mixes symbol collection, REDEFINES/RENAMES resolution, sign propagation, SPECIAL-NAMES parsing, and data hierarchy construction.
ledger.fix: Split into: DataDivisionVisitor, FileControlVisitor, SpecialNamesVisitor, DataSymbolBuilder, ReferenceResolver, SignPropagator. SemanticBuilder becomes coordinator.

ledger.title: Deduplicate procedure name resolution methods
ledger.description: Three near-identical methods: ResolveProcedureName, ResolveProcedureNameForThruEnd, ResolveProcedureNameForPerform with 95% code duplication.
ledger.fix: Extract enum ProcedureResolutionMode { Default, ThruEnd, Perform }. Single method dispatches on mode.

ledger.title: Resolve grammar/string-parsing dualism in preprocessor
ledger.description: CobolPreprocessor.g4 defines COPY/REPLACE grammar but actual preprocessing uses ad-hoc string parsing in CopyProcessor.cs. Two parallel parsing paths create confusion.
ledger.fix: Either remove unused grammar (simpler) or use ANTLR visitor for preprocessing (more robust). Document the decision.

ledger.title: Cache CIL runtime method references
ledger.description: 146 GetMethod/GetConstructor reflection calls scattered throughout CilEmitter. Magic type arrays repeated 50+ times.
ledger.fix: Extract RuntimeSignatures static class with cached Type[] arrays. Create ResolveRuntimeMethod() helper with FrozenDictionary cache.

ledger.title: Modernize switch statements to switch expressions
ledger.description: Heavy use of switch statements over types where switch expressions with exhaustiveness checking would be more appropriate. Affects CilEmitter (50+ case dispatch), BoundTreeValidator, Binder.
ledger.fix: Convert switch-on-type statements to switch expressions where return values or assignments exist. Adds compiler-enforced exhaustiveness.

ledger.title: Preprocessor error reporting via DiagnosticBag
ledger.description: CopyProcessor silently replaces missing copybooks with comments. No diagnostics pipeline. NistPreprocessor has no error reporting.
ledger.fix: Return Result<string, List<Diagnostic>> from preprocessor methods. Thread diagnostics through pipeline. Integrate with DiagnosticBag.

ledger.title: Refactor literal state machine in ReferenceFormatProcessor
ledger.description: ScanLiteralState/HandleContinuation use mutable ref booleans for complex quoted-string state machine. Duplicated literal scanning logic.
ledger.fix: Extract LiteralStateMachine type with enum LiteralState { Outside, InsidePendingClose, Inside }. Immutable state transitions.

ledger.title: NistPreprocessor data-driven placeholder mappings
ledger.description: 24 hardcoded string .Replace() calls with magic XXXXX placeholder codes. Difficult to extend or test.
ledger.fix: Create record NistPlaceholder(string Code, string DefaultValue, string Description). Use data-driven array of placeholders.

ledger.title: Scope resolution caching
ledger.description: Scope.Resolve(name) walks parent chain on every lookup with no caching. Called repeatedly in hot paths (ReferenceResolver, BoundTreeBuilder).
ledger.fix: Cache resolution results in Scope._cache dictionary. Invalidate on TryDeclare only.

ledger.title: ProcedureGraph typed CFG with BasicBlock
ledger.description: ProcedureGraph uses raw Dictionary<string, HashSet<string>> for edges. BasicBlock type exists but is unused in graph construction.
ledger.fix: Migrate ProcedureGraph to emit typed ControlFlowGraph with BasicBlock nodes and Successors/Predecessors lists.

ledger.title: Consolidate StorageHelpers into PicRuntime
ledger.description: StorageHelpers and PicRuntime both handle MOVE with overlapping logic. Acknowledged as TODO in comments.
ledger.fix: Merge StorageHelpers methods into PicRuntime as overloads. Keep only file I/O in StorageHelpers.

ledger.title: Re-enable CI/CD with .NET 9
ledger.description: GitHub Actions CI disabled. Specifies dotnet-version 8.0.x but project targets net9.0.
ledger.fix: Re-enable workflow, update to .NET 9, integrate NIST test suite into CI pipeline.

ledger.title: Add code analyzers (StyleCop, NetAnalyzers)
ledger.description: No AnalysisLevel, EnableNETAnalyzers, or StyleCop configuration. No .editorconfig.
ledger.fix: Add StyleCopAnalyzers and Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers packages. Create .editorconfig.

ledger.title: Subdivide Semantics namespace (20 files)
ledger.description: CobolSharp.Compiler.Semantics has 20 files in a single namespace. Should subdivide into Symbols, Validation, Resolution.
ledger.fix: Create subdirectories with focused namespaces. Move files accordingly.

ledger.title: BoundPerformStatement subtype explosion
ledger.description: BoundPerformStatement has 8 optional properties describing different PERFORM forms. Null-checking determines which form at every consumer site.
ledger.fix: Create sealed subclasses: BoundPerformSimple, BoundPerformTimes, BoundPerformInline, BoundPerformVarying.

ledger.title: FileStateValidator string-keyed state → typed FileState
ledger.description: Uses HashSet<string> and Dictionary<string, string> for open files and pending status checks. No type safety.
ledger.fix: Create record FileState(string FileName, bool IsOpen, string? StatusVariable). Use file symbols instead of strings.

ledger.title: Implement SSA form for IR
ledger.description: Convert the existing IR basic-block representation into Static Single Assignment form with phi-nodes at join points. SSA is the canonical prerequisite for all modern optimization passes — without it, data-flow analysis operates on ambiguous definitions. Touches IrInstruction, IrBasicBlock, and the Binder's lowering output. Requires introducing IrPhiNode instruction type, dominance frontier computation, and variable renaming pass.
deliverables: SSA destruction pass (phi elimination before CIL emission)
Unit tests for SSA properties (single-def, dominator coverage)
risks: COBOL's PERFORM THRU creates irreducible control flow that complicates dominance computation
MOVE to multiple targets creates implicit fan-out that must map correctly to SSA defs
Performance regression if SSA construction is not O(n) in block count
test_impact: New unit test suite for SSA invariants
Existing integration + NIST tests must remain green (SSA is transparent to output)

ledger.title: Implement data-flow analysis framework
ledger.description: Build a generic data-flow analysis framework over SSA-form IR supporting forward and backward analyses. Initial analyses: live variable analysis (backward) and reaching definitions (forward). These are the two foundational analyses required by dead code elimination and constant propagation. The framework should be parameterized by lattice type and transfer functions to support future analyses (available expressions, very busy expressions, etc.).
deliverables: Generic DataFlowAnalysis<TLattice> framework with worklist algorithm
Per-block in/out sets accessible via analysis results
Unit tests verifying analysis correctness on hand-crafted CFGs
risks: COBOL's flat paragraph model (no structured scoping) creates large live ranges
EXTERNAL and LINKAGE items have unbounded lifetimes that must be conservatively handled
Analysis must handle PERFORM THRU's non-structured control flow correctly
test_impact: New unit test suite for data-flow analysis results
No impact on existing tests (analysis is read-only)

ledger.title: Implement dead code elimination
ledger.description: Remove unreachable basic blocks, dead stores (writes to variables never subsequently read), and unused computations from the IR. Uses live variable analysis from M101 to identify dead definitions. Must respect COBOL semantics where DISPLAY and file I/O have observable side effects and must never be eliminated. The pass runs after SSA construction and before CIL emission.
deliverables: Dead store elimination pass (respects side effects)
Unit tests for each elimination category
risks: COBOL programs often have intentionally unreachable paragraphs used only via ALTER — must not eliminate them
File I/O side effects must be precisely classified to avoid incorrect elimination
SIZE ERROR handlers create implicit liveness that must be tracked
test_impact: NIST tests must remain green (optimization must not change observable behavior)

ledger.title: Implement constant folding and propagation
ledger.description: Evaluate constant expressions at compile time (folding) and propagate known constant values through the IR (propagation). Uses reaching definitions from M101 to determine when a variable has a single constant reaching definition at a use site. Must handle COBOL's decimal arithmetic precisely — constant folding must produce the same results as runtime PicRuntime methods. Enables further optimizations like branch elimination when conditions fold to constants.
deliverables: Constant folding pass for IrExpression trees
Constant propagation pass over SSA IR
Decimal precision preservation (must match PicRuntime semantics)
Unit tests for folding correctness and propagation chains
risks: COBOL decimal arithmetic has specific truncation and rounding rules that must be exactly replicated
PIC-edited fields have format-dependent storage that complicates constant representation
REDEFINES creates aliasing that can invalidate constant assumptions

ledger.title: Implement COBOL-2002 intrinsic functions
ledger.description: Extend the existing 94-function intrinsic dispatch with COBOL-2002 additions: FORMATTED-DATE, FORMATTED-TIME, FORMATTED-DATETIME, LOCALE-DATE, LOCALE-TIME, TEST-DATE-YYYYMMDD, TEST-DAY-YYYYDDD, BOOLEAN-OF-INTEGER, INTEGER-OF-BOOLEAN, COMBINED-DATETIME, SECONDS-FROM-FORMATTED-TIME, and others. The grammar already reserves these names; the work is in the Runtime.Intrinsics dispatch and argument validation in the binder.
deliverables: Runtime implementations for ~20 new intrinsic functions
Binder argument count/type validation per ISO spec
Unit tests for each new function
Integration tests with COBOL source exercising new functions
risks: Locale-dependent functions require .NET globalization APIs that may behave differently cross-platform
Date/time format strings in COBOL differ from .NET format strings
Some functions require calendar arithmetic (Julian day, leap year) with edge cases
test_impact: New integration tests with COBOL source

ledger.title: Implement COBOL-2002 object-oriented features
ledger.description: Implement CLASS-ID, METHOD-ID, FACTORY, OBJECT, INVOKE, and the COBOL-2002 object model. This is the single largest feature addition possible — it touches every compiler layer from grammar (new division structure) through semantics (class/method symbol resolution, inheritance, interface conformance) through code generation (mapping to .NET class hierarchies). Requires new bound node types (BoundClassDefinition, BoundMethodDefinition, BoundInvokeStatement) and new IR instructions for virtual dispatch.
deliverables: Grammar rules for CLASS-ID, METHOD-ID, FACTORY, OBJECT paragraphs
ClassSymbol, MethodSymbol, InterfaceSymbol in semantic model
IR instructions for object creation and virtual dispatch
CIL emission mapping COBOL classes to .NET classes
Unit + integration tests for OO programs
risks: COBOL's OO model differs significantly from .NET's (no constructors, factory paragraphs, conformance vs inheritance)
INVOKE dispatches to paragraphs within methods, not to .NET methods directly
Multiple inheritance via interfaces requires careful .NET mapping
This is estimated at 2000+ lines of new code across all layers

ledger.title: Implement semantic verifier pass
ledger.description: Create a structured post-binding validation pass that enforces the full set of ISO COBOL semantic rules. Currently, type checking and validation are scattered across BoundTreeBuilder (now decomposed into binders) and BoundTreeValidator. This item consolidates them into a single, exhaustive SemanticVerifier that walks the bound tree and checks: MOVE type compatibility matrix, arithmetic operand types, file OPEN/CLOSE state consistency, PERFORM overlap detection, index/subscript range validity, and REDEFINES/RENAMES constraint enforcement.
deliverables: SemanticVerifier class with exhaustive rule checking
Diagnostic codes for each rule violation (mapped to ISO spec sections)
File state consistency checking across procedure divisions
Unit tests for each rule category
risks: Some ISO rules are context-dependent and require flow analysis (e.g., file state)
PERFORM overlap detection is O(n²) in paragraph count without optimization
False positives on valid but unusual COBOL patterns could block compilation
test_impact: May surface new diagnostics on existing NIST programs (expected, not regressions)

ledger.title: Implement optimized CIL backend
ledger.description: Optimize the CIL output for runtime performance: minimize evaluation stack depth (avoid unnecessary stloc/ldloc pairs), reuse local variable slots across non-overlapping lifetimes, inline small paragraph methods (< 32 IL bytes), and optimize Mono.Cecil metadata (string deduplication, method table compaction). Currently every IR value gets its own CIL local, creating unnecessarily large method bodies. Stack scheduling alone could reduce local count by 40-60%.
deliverables: Stack depth scheduler (peephole optimization for stloc/ldloc elimination)
Local variable slot allocator (register allocation over CIL locals)
Small-method inliner for trivially short paragraphs
Benchmark suite comparing before/after IL size and runtime performance
risks: Stack scheduling changes IL structure — must verify PDB/debugging still works
Method inlining changes PERFORM dispatch semantics (must preserve ALTER behavior)
Local reuse requires accurate liveness data (depends on M101 or a simpler CIL-level analysis)
test_impact: All existing tests must remain green

ledger.title: Implement WASM backend
ledger.description: Add WebAssembly as a second compilation target alongside .NET CIL. Requires abstracting the current CIL-specific EmissionContext and emitter classes behind a backend interface (IBackend or similar), then implementing a WasmEmitter that produces .wasm modules. The COBOL runtime (PicRuntime, StorageHelpers, FileRuntime) must be ported or compiled to WASM as well. Primary use case: running COBOL programs in browsers or serverless WASM runtimes.
deliverables: IBackend abstraction over CIL and WASM emission
COBOL runtime compiled to WASM (via .NET WASI or manual port)
End-to-end test: compile COBOL → WASM → run in wasmtime
risks: WASM lacks decimal arithmetic natively — must ship a decimal library
File I/O in WASM requires WASI or custom host functions
COBOL's byte-array storage model maps poorly to WASM's linear memory without careful design
Runtime size may be prohibitive for browser deployment

ledger.title: Implement incremental compilation and caching
ledger.description: Add file-level incremental compilation with a persistent cache. Track source file content hashes, COPY book dependencies, and compilation timestamps to avoid recompiling unchanged programs. For multi-program projects, build a dependency graph (CALL targets → source files) and recompile only the transitive closure of changed files. Cache bound trees and IR modules to accelerate re-compilation. Integrates with the CLI and future IDE tooling.
deliverables: Source file dependency tracker (COPY book graph)
Persistent compilation cache (bound tree + IR serialization)
Unit tests for cache invalidation correctness
risks: COPY book resolution is context-dependent (REPLACING changes content)
Dynamic CALL targets cannot be statically resolved — must conservatively recompile
Cache serialization format must be versioned to handle compiler upgrades
Race conditions in parallel builds if cache is shared

ledger.title: Implement IDE language service
ledger.description: Build an LSP-compatible language service that exposes the compiler's syntax tree, semantic model, and diagnostics to IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains). Features: real-time diagnostics, hover information (data item type, PIC, storage location), go-to-definition for paragraphs/data items, find-all-references, code completion for reserved words and data names, and document outline (division/section/paragraph structure). Requires making the parser and semantic model incrementally updatable.
deliverables: Hover provider (PIC, type, storage offset, level)
Go-to-definition for paragraphs, sections, data items
Code completion (reserved words, data names, paragraph names)
risks: Incremental parsing is significantly harder than batch parsing for ANTLR4
COBOL's reference format (columns 1-6/7/8-72/73-80) makes character-level editing complex
Semantic model must handle incomplete/invalid programs gracefully
LSP protocol has edge cases around COBOL's case-insensitivity

ledger.title: Implement performance instrumentation
ledger.description: Add compile-time performance instrumentation to every pipeline stage: preprocessing, lexing, parsing, semantic analysis, binding, lowering, and CIL emission. Track wall-clock time, IR node counts, basic block counts, instruction counts, and memory allocation per stage. Output as structured JSON for automated analysis. Enables identification of compilation bottlenecks and regression detection in CI.
deliverables: IR size metrics (node count, block count, instruction count per method)
JSON metrics output (--metrics flag on CLI)
Baseline metrics for NIST test suite
CI integration for performance regression detection
risks: Timer granularity on Windows vs Linux may differ
GC allocation tracking adds overhead — must be opt-in
Metrics collection must not affect compilation correctness

ledger.title: Implement full diagnostic suite
ledger.description: Systematize the existing 200+ diagnostic codes (COBOL0001-COBOL0600, CBL0601-CBL3606) into a complete, documented catalog. Each diagnostic gets: unique code, severity (error/warning/info), category (syntax/semantic/codegen/runtime), human-readable message template, detailed help text with ISO spec citation, and suggested fix. Add a --explain flag to the CLI that prints the full help text for any diagnostic code. Ensure every code path that emits a diagnostic uses a registered descriptor.
deliverables: Per-diagnostic help text with ISO spec citations
Diagnostic category enum (Syntax, Semantic, CodeGen, Runtime)
Audit pass ensuring no raw string diagnostics remain
risks: Some existing diagnostics may have incorrect severity or category
Help text authoring is labor-intensive (~200 entries)
ISO spec citations require careful cross-referencing
test_impact: New diagnostic catalog tests (every code has help text)

ledger.title: COBOL-85 grammar conformance audit
ledger.description: Full audit of the ANTLR grammar against the COBOL-85 standard (ISO/IEC 1989:1985), including all syntactic forms, optional phrases, and IBM/MF extensions already gated. Ensures every production rule is present, correctly structured, and unambiguous. GRAMMAR_AUDIT.md identified ~56 remaining COBOL-85 gaps out of ~138 originally found. This item closes all remaining gaps and produces a verified conformance matrix mapping each spec section to its grammar rule. Covers all four divisions plus COPY/REPLACE preprocessing directives.
deliverables: Conformance matrix: spec section → grammar rule → status
All ~56 remaining COBOL-85 grammar gaps closed
ANTLR grammar compiles with zero warnings
No SLL prediction failures on NIST corpus
risks: Some spec constructs may be genuinely ambiguous in LL(*) — may require lexer mode workarounds
CURRENCY SIGN WITH PICTURE SYMBOL is blocked by PICMODE lexer architecture
Fixing one rule may introduce ambiguity in another (ANTLR precedence sensitivity)
test_impact: New grammar-level parse tests for each fixed gap
NIST test suite may unlock additional passing programs

ledger.title: COBOL-85 binder coverage audit
ledger.description: Systematic verification that every COBOL-85 statement, clause, operand form, and edge case is bound correctly by the decomposed binder classes (M004). Covers all PERFORM variants (simple, TIMES, VARYING, THRU, inline, TEST BEFORE/AFTER), SEARCH/SEARCH ALL, ALTER, USE declaratives, ACCEPT (all sources), STRING/UNSTRING/INSPECT (all forms), all arithmetic statements (ADD/SUBTRACT/MULTIPLY/DIVIDE/COMPUTE with GIVING/REMAINDER/CORRESPONDING/SIZE ERROR), and all file I/O statements. Each binder class gets an exhaustive audit against the spec.
deliverables: Per-binder audit report (9 binders × spec coverage matrix)
Missing binding paths identified and implemented
Edge case tests for each statement form
Binder-level unit tests for untested paths
Updated decomposition docs with coverage status
risks: Some statement forms may parse correctly but produce incorrect bound trees
Abbreviated conditions in EVALUATE WHEN may have untested edge cases
CORRESPONDING matching rules have subtle scoping semantics
test_impact: New integration tests for edge cases

ledger.title: COBOL-85 condition and expression semantics
ledger.description: Ensures all abbreviated conditions, relational operators, sign/class tests, figurative constants, and arithmetic precedence rules match the COBOL-85 spec exactly. Abbreviated combined relation conditions (§6.3.4.2) are particularly complex — the current ExpandAbbrev implementation must be verified against all spec examples. Sign conditions (POSITIVE/NEGATIVE/ZERO) must respect the distinction between operational sign and algebraic sign. Class conditions (NUMERIC/ALPHABETIC/ALPHABETIC-LOWER/ALPHABETIC-UPPER) must check against the correct character sets per the program's collating sequence.
deliverables: Abbreviated condition expansion verified against all spec examples
Sign condition semantics verified (operational vs algebraic)
Figurative constant promotion rules documented and tested
Arithmetic precedence rules verified (left-to-right within same level)
risks: Abbreviated conditions with nested NOT have spec-ambiguous interpretations
Collating sequence affects class condition results — NATIVE vs STANDARD-1
Some NIST tests exercise rare combinations not yet encountered

ledger.title: COBOL-85 file I/O semantics
ledger.description: Full validation of OPEN/CLOSE/READ/WRITE/REWRITE/DELETE/START/RETURN semantics against the COBOL-85 standard. Includes file access mode enforcement (sequential/random/dynamic), record locking semantics (LOCK/NO LOCK), key handling for indexed files (primary, alternate, DUPLICATES), AT END/INVALID KEY/NOT AT END/NOT INVALID KEY imperative routing, FILE STATUS code generation per §9.1.12, and USE AFTER EXCEPTION/ERROR declarative invocation. The runtime (FileRuntime) must produce the correct 2-character status codes for every I/O condition.
deliverables: File access mode enforcement in binder
Record locking semantics (CLOSE WITH LOCK, etc.)
Key handling for indexed/relative files verified
FILE STATUS code matrix (all 2-character codes per spec)
File I/O integration tests for each access mode × operation combination
risks: Some file operations have mode-dependent behavior that varies by organization
Sequential vs dynamic access mode affects which operations are legal
OPTIONAL files have special OPEN behavior that must be tested
test_impact: NIST IX/SQ suite tests may become passable

ledger.title: COBOL-85 control flow semantics
ledger.description: Ensures PERFORM, GO TO, ALTER, EVALUATE, IF, and declaratives behave exactly as defined in the COBOL-85 standard. PERFORM range overlap detection (§14.8.35) must detect and diagnose when two active PERFORMs share overlapping paragraph ranges. EVALUATE matching rules (§14.8.14) must correctly implement the subject/object comparison matrix including TRUE/FALSE subjects, THRU ranges, ANY, and condition-name objects. GO TO DEPENDING ON must handle out-of-range values correctly (fall through). ALTER must modify only the first GO TO in the targeted paragraph.
deliverables: PERFORM range overlap detection and diagnostic
EVALUATE matching matrix verified against all spec examples
GO TO DEPENDING out-of-range behavior verified
ALTER semantics verified (first GO TO only)
Control flow integration tests for each edge case
risks: PERFORM overlap detection requires runtime tracking, not just static analysis
EVALUATE with mixed TRUE/FALSE subjects and condition-name objects is underspecified in edge cases
ALTER is rarely used but must work correctly when present

ledger.title: COBOL-85 data division semantics
ledger.description: Full validation of PIC clauses, USAGE, REDEFINES, RENAMES, OCCURS, INDEXED BY, SYNCHRONIZED, JUSTIFIED, BLANK WHEN ZERO, and numeric category rules against the COBOL-85 standard. PIC clause validation must enforce the character count limits, implicit decimal point placement, and valid editing combinations per §13.16. REDEFINES must enforce the same-level, same-or-smaller-size, and no-OCCURS-DEPENDING constraints. OCCURS DEPENDING ON must validate the controlling item is a valid integer data item. RENAMES (level 66) must enforce the contiguous subordinate item constraint.
deliverables: PIC clause validation against all spec rules
OCCURS DEPENDING ON controlling item validation
USAGE compatibility matrix (COMP, COMP-3, DISPLAY, INDEX)
Data division unit tests for each constraint
risks: PIC editing rules have many valid combinations that are hard to enumerate exhaustively
SYNCHRONIZED alignment depends on machine word size (platform-dependent)
OCCURS DEPENDING ON with REDEFINES creates complex storage layout rules
test_impact: Existing NIST NC101A/NC109A/NC110A coverage may expand

ledger.title: COBOL-85 runtime behavior conformance
ledger.description: Ensures runtime behavior for arithmetic, string operations, truncation, rounding, overflow, and SIZE ERROR matches COBOL-85 semantics exactly. Arithmetic must use decimal (not floating-point) with intermediate results retaining maximum precision per §6.4.1. Truncation rules differ between MOVE (alphanumeric: right-truncate; numeric: left-truncate high-order digits) and arithmetic (SIZE ERROR). Rounding must apply at the last retained digit position. STRING/UNSTRING pointer and overflow behavior must match §14.8.41/§14.8.43. INSPECT TALLYING/REPLACING/CONVERTING must process left-to-right with non-overlapping matches.
deliverables: Arithmetic precision rules verified (intermediate result size)
Truncation rules verified for MOVE and arithmetic separately
Rounding verified at correct digit position
SIZE ERROR detection verified for all arithmetic operations
STRING/UNSTRING pointer and overflow behavior verified
INSPECT left-to-right non-overlapping match behavior verified
Runtime unit tests for each behavior category
risks: Intermediate arithmetic precision rules are complex and implementation-defined in some cases
Some truncation edge cases depend on PIC category interactions
STRING overflow detection timing (before vs after pointer update) must match spec exactly
test_impact: NIST arithmetic and string tests may see improvements

ledger.title: COBOL-85 NIST test suite completion
ledger.description: Achieve 100% passing on all 95 NIST COBOL-85 kernel tests (NC series) with zero FAIL* results locked in baselines. Currently 95 tests pass with 77 FAIL* results accepted in valid baselines. This item eliminates all 77 FAIL* results by fixing the underlying compiler/runtime bugs. Also expands to non-NC suites (IC, IF, IX, SQ, ST, etc.) that test inter-program communication, intrinsic functions, indexed files, sequential files, and sorting. The guard script must report zero regressions and zero FAIL* on the full suite.
deliverables: All 95 NC-series tests pass with zero FAIL* in baselines
IX suite (indexed file I/O) tests passing
SQ suite (sequential file I/O) tests passing
Guard script updated for expanded test suite
NIST conformance report documenting pass/fail/skip for all programs
risks: Some FAIL* results may require significant runtime changes to fix
Non-NC suites may expose entirely new categories of bugs
NC220M/NC237A runtime hangs are still undiagnosed
Some NIST tests may test implementation-defined behavior
test_impact: Non-NC test suites added to guard script
Total NIST program count increases from 95 to potentially 200+

ledger.title: COBOL-85 extension gating (IBM/MF)
ledger.description: Ensure all IBM and Micro Focus extensions are properly gated behind dialect flags and do not interfere with pure COBOL-85 mode. Currently some extensions (COMP-5, service-reload, XML PARSE) are unconditionally accepted by the grammar. Each extension must be documented, categorized (IBM-only, MF-only, common), and controlled by a CompilationOptions dialect flag. In strict COBOL-85 mode, any extension syntax must produce a diagnostic. The grammar audit (M200) identifies which rules are extensions vs standard.
deliverables: Dialect enum: Strict85, IBM, MicroFocus, Permissive
Each extension rule gated by dialect check in binder
Diagnostic for extension syntax in Strict85 mode
Extension catalog documenting each gated feature
Unit tests verifying dialect gating for each extension
risks: Some 'extensions' are so widely used they may be required for practical COBOL programs
Gating at the grammar level vs binder level has different trade-offs (parse errors vs semantic errors)
IBM and MF extensions sometimes conflict with each other
test_impact: NIST tests must pass in Strict85 mode

ledger.title: COBOL-85 compliance certification
ledger.description: Final verification pass, documentation, and certification that the compiler is fully COBOL-85 compliant. Produces a formal compliance document mapping each section of ISO/IEC 1989:1985 to the compiler's implementation status. Includes a compliance matrix, a list of implementation-defined behaviors with their chosen values, a list of any intentional deviations (with justification), and a summary of NIST test results. This is the capstone item for the COBOL-85 compliance initiative.
deliverables: Intentional deviation document (with justification for each)
risks: Some spec sections may be genuinely unimplementable on .NET (e.g., SEGMENT classification)
Implementation-defined behaviors must be chosen carefully for IBM/MF compatibility
Compliance claims require ongoing maintenance as the compiler evolves
test_impact: No new tests (this is a documentation + verification item)
All existing tests must be green

ledger.title: Close remaining COBOL-85 grammar gaps (26 verified remaining)

Batch 3 closed final 2 REJECT gaps (M407, M411). ALL 12 REJECT grammar gaps now closed. 15 OVERLENIENT gaps (P3) remain. 9 SEMANTIC-ONLY gaps remain (M302/M304/M310). 5 sub-gaps from Batch 3 design (M429-M433). LPAREN subscript trigger refactored to production-quality _dataNameTokens HashSet whitelist.
deliverables: Add positive and negative parser tests for each fix
Update GRAMMAR_AUDIT.md to reflect 0 open COBOL-85 items
risks: Grammar changes can break existing 95 NC + 421 unit + 274 integration tests
PICMODE lexer architecture blocks CURRENCY SIGN WITH PICTURE SYMBOL (M407)
SCREEN SECTION (M411) is an entire new grammar section — large scope
test_impact: Parser unit tests for each new/modified construct
Regression runs of all NC and future non-NC NIST suites
guard.sh must pass after each batch
